source: CIS GOOGLE CLOUD PLATFORM FOUNDATION BENCHMARK V4.0.0
title: Ensure That Cloud Audit Logging Is Configured Properly

input_row.source: CIS GOOGLE CLOUD PLATFORM FOUNDATION BENCHMARK V4.0.0
input_row.title: Ensure That Cloud Audit Logging Is Configured Properly
input_row.description: It is recommended that Cloud Audit Logging is configured to track all admin activities and read, write access to user data.
input_row.rationale: Cloud Audit Logging maintains two audit logs for each project, folder, and organization: Admin Activity and Data Access. 1. Admin Activity logs contain log entries for API calls or other administrative actions that modify the configuration or metadata of resources. Admin Activity audit logs are enabled for all services and cannot be configured. 2. Data Access audit logs record API calls that create, modify, or read user- provided data. These are disabled by default and should be enabled. There are three kinds of Data Access audit log information: o Admin read: Records operations that read metadata or configuration information. Admin Activity audit logs record writes of metadata and configuration information that cannot be disabled. o Data read: Records operations that read user-provided data. o Data write: Records operations that write user-provided data. It is recommended to have an effective default audit config configured in such a way that: 1. logtype is set to DATA_READ (to log user activity tracking) and DATA_WRITES (to log changes/tampering to user data). 2. audit config is enabled for all the services supported by the Data Access audit logs feature. 3. Logs should be captured for all users, i.e., there are no exempted users in any of the audit config sections. This will ensure overriding the audit config will not contradict the requirement. Impact: There is no charge for Admin Activity audit logs. Enabling the Data Access audit logs might result in your project being charged for the additional logs usage.
input_row.audit: From Google Cloud Console 1. Go to Audit Logs by visiting https://console.cloud.google.com/iam-admin/audit. 2. Ensure that Admin Read, Data Write, and Data Read are enabled for all Google Cloud services and that no exemptions are allowed. From Google Cloud CLI 1. List the Identity and Access Management (IAM) policies for the project, folder, or organization: gcloud organizations get-iam-policy ORGANIZATION_ID gcloud resource-manager folders get-iam-policy FOLDER_ID gcloud projects get-iam-policy PROJECT_ID 2. Policy should have a default auditConfigs section which has the logtype set to DATA_WRITES and DATA_READ for all services. Note that projects inherit settings from folders, which in turn inherit settings from the organization. When called, projects get-iam-policy, the result shows only the policies set in the project, not the policies inherited from the parent folder or organization. Nevertheless, if the parent folder has Cloud Audit Logging enabled, the project does as well. Sample output for default audit configs may look like this: auditConfigs: - auditLogConfigs: - logType: ADMIN_READ - logType: DATA_WRITE - logType: DATA_READ service: allServices 3. Any of the auditConfigs sections should not have parameter "exemptedMembers:" set, which will ensure that Logging is enabled for all users and no user is exempted.
input_row.remediation: From Google Cloud Console 1. Go to Audit Logs by visiting https://console.cloud.google.com/iam-admin/audit. 2. Follow the steps at https://cloud.google.com/logging/docs/audit/configure-data- access to enable audit logs for all Google Cloud services. Ensure that no exemptions are allowed.  From Google Cloud CLI 1. To read the project's IAM policy and store it in a file run a command: gcloud projects get-iam-policy PROJECT_ID > /tmp/project_policy.yaml Alternatively, the policy can be set at the organization or folder level. If setting the policy at the organization level, it is not necessary to also set it for each folder or project. gcloud organizations get-iam-policy ORGANIZATION_ID > /tmp/org_policy.yaml gcloud resource-manager folders get-iam-policy FOLDER_ID > /tmp/folder_policy.yaml 2. Edit policy in /tmp/policy.yaml, adding or changing only the audit logs configuration to: Note: Admin Activity Logs are enabled by default, and cannot be disabled. So they are not listed in these configuration changes. auditConfigs: - auditLogConfigs: - logType: DATA_WRITE - logType: DATA_READ service: allServices Note: exemptedMembers: is not set as audit logging should be enabled for all the users 3. To write new IAM policy run command: gcloud organizations set-iam-policy ORGANIZATION_ID /tmp/org_policy.yaml gcloud resource-manager folders set-iam-policy FOLDER_ID /tmp/folder_policy.yaml gcloud projects set-iam-policy PROJECT_ID /tmp/project_policy.yaml If the preceding command reports a conflict with another change, then repeat these steps, starting with the first step. Default Value: Admin Activity logs are always enabled. They cannot be disabled. Data Access audit logs are disabled by default because they can be quite large. References: 1. https://cloud.google.com/logging/docs/audit/ 2. https://cloud.google.com/logging/docs/audit/configure-data-access Additional Information: • Log type DATA_READ is equally important to that of DATA_WRITE to track detailed user activities. • BigQuery Data Access logs are handled differently from other data access logs. BigQuery logs are enabled by default and cannot be disabled. They do not count against logs allotment and cannot result in extra logs charges.
input_row.impact: There is no charge for Admin Activity audit logs. Enabling the Data Access audit logs might result in your project being charged for the additional logs usage.
input_row.references: 1. https://cloud.google.com/logging/docs/audit/ 2. https://cloud.google.com/logging/docs/audit/configure-data-access Additional Information: • Log type DATA_READ is equally important to that of DATA_WRITE to track detailed user activities. • BigQuery Data Access logs are handled differently from other data access logs. BigQuery logs are enabled by default and cannot be disabled. They do not count against logs allotment and cannot result in extra logs charges.

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Automated

**APPROACH DECISION:**
The control can be fully verified via GCP APIs/Google Cloud SDK (gcloud)/GCP CLI because it involves checking IAM policy settings and audit configurations, which are accessible and queryable using GCP APIs.

**PROGRAM NAME:** gcp_logging_project_audit_logging_configured_properly

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:**
- Use the Google Cloud SDK (gcloud) to retrieve IAM policies and audit configurations for projects, folders, or organizations.
- Relevant GCP CLI commands include:
  - `gcloud organizations get-iam-policy ORGANIZATION_ID`
  - `gcloud resource-manager folders get-iam-policy FOLDER_ID`
  - `gcloud projects get-iam-policy PROJECT_ID`
- Check for the presence of `auditConfigs` with `logType` set to `DATA_READ` and `DATA_WRITE` for `allServices`.
- Ensure that the `exemptedMembers` parameter is not set under any `auditConfigs`, indicating that no users are exempted from logging.
- Specific resources to check:
  - Organization, folder, and project-based IAM policies.
- Validate that both `DATA_READ` and `DATA_WRITE` log types are enabled under `auditConfigs` for all services, ensuring comprehensive logging.
- Criteria for Pass/Fail:
  - Pass: `auditConfigs` contain `DATA_READ` and `DATA_WRITE` for `allServices` without `exemptedMembers`.
  - Fail: Missing `DATA_READ` or `DATA_WRITE` in `auditConfigs`, or presence of `exemptedMembers`.

This approach leverages GCP SDK commands to ensure that audit logging is comprehensively configured across the organization, folders, or projects without manual intervention.
step2_review:
---
### 1. APPROACH VALIDATION: **PARTIALLY_AGREE**

- **Manual vs Automated Classification:**  
  The classification as "Automated" is mostly correct. The control can be checked programmatically using GCP APIs and the `gcloud` CLI, as audit logging configuration is exposed via IAM policy objects.
- **GCP SDK/CLI Capability:**  
  The assessment correctly identifies that `gcloud` commands can retrieve IAM policies, including `auditConfigs`. However, the assessment overlooks that **Cloud Audit Logs configuration is not only set at the IAM policy level** but also at the Logging level (Log Sinks, Exclusions, and Log Types enabled in Cloud Logging). The IAM policy's `auditConfigs` only control which activities are logged, not the retention, export, or actual log delivery.
- **Other Considerations:**  
  The assessment does not mention GCP Config (Config Validator or Asset Inventory), which could also be used for automated checks. CloudFormation is not relevant for GCP.

**Summary:** The approach is mostly correct but incomplete, as it focuses only on IAM policy `auditConfigs` and misses other aspects of audit logging configuration.

---

### 2. TECHNICAL ACCURACY: **Score: 7/10**

- **Automation Details:**  
  - The `gcloud` commands listed (`get-iam-policy`) are valid for retrieving IAM policies, including `auditConfigs`.
  - The logic for checking `DATA_READ` and `DATA_WRITE` for `allServices` and ensuring no `exemptedMembers` is correct **for the IAM policy aspect**.
- **Missing/Incomplete Elements:**  
  - The control (per CIS) expects **Cloud Audit Logs to be enabled for all admin activity and data access**. This requires checking that the appropriate log types (ADMIN_READ, DATA_READ, DATA_WRITE) are enabled for all services, and that logs are not excluded or filtered out via Logging Exclusions.
  - The assessment does **not check if logs are actually being ingested and retained** (i.e., that Cloud Logging is not misconfigured, or logs are not being excluded or deleted prematurely).
  - The program name (`gcp_logging_project_audit_logging_configured_properly`) is acceptable, but could be more specific (see Improvements).
- **Program Name Convention:**  
  - The name follows the convention, but could be clearer about the specific aspect being checked (e.g., `iam_auditconfigs` vs. `logging_audit_logs`).

---

### 3. IMPROVEMENTS

- **Expand Scope:**  
  - Include checks for **Cloud Logging Exclusions**: Use `gcloud logging sinks list` and `gcloud logging exclusions list` to ensure no exclusions are preventing audit logs from being ingested.
  - Validate **log retention settings**: Use `gcloud logging buckets describe` to ensure logs are retained for an appropriate period.
  - Consider **Config Validator** or **Asset Inventory** for organization-wide checks.
- **Alternative GCP API Methods:**  
  - Use the [Cloud Asset Inventory API](https://cloud.google.com/asset-inventory/docs/reference/rest/v1/assets/searchAllIamPolicies) to programmatically search for `auditConfigs` across all resources.
- **Program Name Suggestion:**  
  - If only checking IAM policy:  
    `gcp_iam_project_auditconfigs_data_access_logging_enabled`
  - If expanding to logging configuration:  
    `gcp_logging_project_audit_logs_data_access_configured_properly`
- **Missing Considerations:**  
  - The assessment should clarify that **both IAM policy and Cloud Logging configuration** must be checked for full coverage of the CIS control.
  - Should mention checking for **all three log types**: `ADMIN_READ`, `DATA_READ`, and `DATA_WRITE`.
  - Should specify that checks must be performed at **organization, folder, and project** levels, as audit configs can be inherited or overridden.

---

### 4. CONFIDENCE: **MEDIUM**

- The assessment is technically correct for the IAM policy aspect, but **misses critical elements** of the control (actual log delivery, exclusions, retention).
- The automation approach is valid, but **not fully comprehensive** for the CIS requirement.

---

### 5. FINAL RECOMMENDATION: **Modify**

- **Modify** the automation details to:
  - Include checks for Cloud Logging exclusions and retention.
  - Clarify that both IAM policy `auditConfigs` and Cloud Logging configuration must be checked.
  - Update the program name for clarity and specificity.
- **Do not reclassify** as Manual, but note that a comprehensive automated check requires more than just IAM policy inspection.

---

**Summary Table:**

| Section              | Assessment         | Comments                                                                                  |
|----------------------|-------------------|-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
| Approach Validation  | PARTIALLY_AGREE   | Misses Cloud Logging config/exclusions/retention.                                         |
| Technical Accuracy   | 7/10              | Correct for IAM policy, incomplete for full CIS control.                                  |
| Improvements         | See above         | Add logging exclusion/retention checks; clarify log types; update program name.           |
| Confidence           | MEDIUM            | Good for IAM, incomplete for full control.                                                |
| Final Recommendation | Modify            | Expand automation scope and clarify program name.                                         |
